communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: University of Michigan Communications with Grand River Solutions and InCompliance (2024–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I request access to and copies of all public records reflecting communications between employees, agents, or representatives of the University of Michigan and the following individuals and entities:

Omar Torres of Grand River Solutions
Stephanie Jackson of InCompliance Consulting (also known as Beyond Consulting)

for the time period beginning January 1, 2024 through the date of search.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

Emails and email attachments (including all domains used by these individuals or entities)
Internal communications referencing these individuals, entities, or their work
Contracts, scopes of work, engagement letters, and amendments
Invoices, billing records, and payment documentation
Reports, memoranda, summaries, draft documents, and final deliverables
Notes, meeting minutes, calendar entries, and scheduling records
Communications or records stored in cloud or case management systems, including but not limited to Box, Google Drive, and CareNetwork/Symplicity

This request specifically includes records relating to consultation, investigation, or advice concerning:

student conduct
safety or security concerns
social media activity
harassment or threat assessments
Title IX, civil rights, or compliance matters

Please include records maintained in all formats and locations reasonably likely to contain responsive materials.

If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please provide a written explanation identifying:

the specific statutory exemption claimed under MCL 15.243
a description of the record or portion withheld sufficient to permit review of the applicability of the exemption

If responsive records exist in part but are exempt in part, please provide all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

I request a waiver or reduction of fees on the grounds that disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute to a better understanding of the University’s use of external consultants in matters involving student conduct, safety, and civil rights compliance.

If fees are expected to exceed $50, please provide a detailed itemized estimate before processing.

I prefer to receive records in electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Laurel Billings

title: FBI Records — Denise Oliver / Young Lords Organization (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to, mentioning, or describing the following individual:

Full name: Denise Oliver-Velez
Also known as: "Denise Oliver"

SCOPE OF REQUEST

I request all records pertaining to the above-named individual, including but not limited to:

1. All main files, field office files, and headquarters files maintained under any file classification, including COINTELPRO (Counterintelligence Program) files, domestic intelligence files, and any files maintained under the following subject categories: subversive activities, internal security, nationalistic tendency, foreign influence, or racial matters.

2. All records relating to the subject's association with or membership in the Young Lords Organization (YLO), Young Lords Party (YLP), or any related organization, including cross-reference documents, administrative pages, and see-references.

3. All informant reports, source reports, and surveillance logs in which the subject is named, described, or referenced, regardless of whether the subject is the primary subject of the document.

4. All correspondence, memoranda, teletype communications, and inter-agency communications in which the subject is mentioned, including communications between the New York field office and FBI Headquarters, and between the FBI and the NYPD, the Puerto Rico field office, the CIA, NSA, or any other federal, state, or local agency.

5. All records resulting from physical surveillance, including surveillance logs, photographic records, and written reports of observed activities.

6. All bank account records, financial surveillance records, and records of mail covers or mail openings relating to the subject.

7. All records relating to any criminal charges, arrests, or prosecutions in which the FBI played a role or maintained an interest relating to the subject.

DATE RANGE

January 1, 1968 through December 31, 1975, with particular emphasis on December 1968 through December 1972.

ELECTRONIC SURVEILLANCE AND AUDIO/VISUAL RECORDS

In addition to the records above, I specifically request the following:

8. ELSUR Index search: Please conduct a thorough search of the FBI's Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Index for any entries naming or referencing the above-named subject, and produce: (a) all ELSUR index entries; (b) all coverage logs and monitoring reports; (c) any transcripts or summaries of intercepted communications; and (d) any administrative memoranda authorizing or documenting the surveillance.

9. Audio recordings: All audio recordings — including wire recordings, magnetic tape, reel-to-reel, cassette, or digital audio — made in connection with any surveillance of the above-named subject, whether pursuant to court-authorized wiretaps, microphone installations, consensual monitoring by informants or undercover agents, or any other means of audio capture.

10. Video and film recordings: All motion picture film, videotape, or digital video recordings made in connection with physical surveillance of the above-named subject.

11. Photographic records: All still photographs of the above-named subject contained in any FBI file, including surveillance photographs, identification photographs, and photographs attached to informant reports.

12. Cross-agency ELSUR records: All records indicating that electronic surveillance of the above-named subject was conducted by or shared with the NYPD Bureau of Special Services (Red Squad), the CIA, the NSA, or any other agency, including copies of inter-agency communications relating to such surveillance.

13. Technical surveillance (TESUR) and microphone coverage (MISUR): All records relating to technical surveillance or microphone coverage operations targeting the above-named subject or any location known to be frequented by the subject, including Young Lords offices, meeting spaces, or residences.

If any responsive audio or video records have been destroyed, please provide documentation of the destruction, including date, method, authorization, and the retention schedule under which destruction was authorized.

PUBLIC INTEREST STATEMENT

The requested records will be made available to the general public and are being sought for documentary film research purposes. This request is not being made for commercial purposes. The subject matter — federal surveillance of Puerto Rican civil rights organizations — is of significant historical and public interest, and disclosure is likely to contribute substantially to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kristofer Ríos

title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Mount Vernon Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Mount Vernon Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ## 

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ## 

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production. 

## FEE REQUEST ## 

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced. 

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request: 

Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles 
(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered 
but vehicles are believed to remain in service): 
c. 50816D — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010) 
d. 50887D — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031) 
e. 54180D — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031) 
f. 43500D — Bomb squad trailer (2005) 
g. 26073D — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997) 
h. 41461D — Communications van (2005)

Other:

b. 826-ZVA 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Full List of Custodians of Records for City of St Louis
communications.communication:
March 27, 2026
 
City of St. Louis
City Counselor’s Office
1520 Market Street
Room 4025
St. Louis, MO 63103
 
Custodian of Records/ Chief Legal Counsel,

This is a request for records under the Missouri Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, Revised Statutes of Missouri.

Section 610.023.1, RSMo, provides that a public governmental body is to appoint a custodian to maintain that body’s records and the identity and location of the custodian is to be made available upon request; and
Section 610.026, RSMo, sets forth that a public governmental body shall provide access to and, upon request, furnish copies of public records; and
Section 610.028.2, RSMo, provides that a public governmental body shall provide a reasonable written policy in compliance with sections 610.010 to 610.030, RSMo, commonly referred to as the Sunshine Law, regarding the release of information on any meeting, record or vote.
 
Requested Documents/Records:

I request that you provide copies of:

All documents/records sufficient to clearly identify the names and locations of the most current appointed custodians of records for all agencies, departments, sections, boards and commissions of the City of St Louis, Missouri.

Please include the custodian’s first/last name, title, physical location with room/suite, street address (for postal service), city, state, email address and direct phone number with extension.

I am respectfully seeking all documents/records through MuckRock.com.

I am requesting that documents/records be sent electronically, preferably as a searchable PDF file attachment or any other digital formats in which they exist (such a CSV or Excel).

If you are unable to fill the request electronically, please advise me of other alternatives.
 
Fee Waiver/Public Interest/Public Release:

As the records sought detail the operations and activities of local government, I also request that fees be waived as this request is in the public’s interest. I am seeking the records to analyze, inform and freely release to members of the public as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com.

As stated in Section 610.022, RSMo, “public records shall be presumed to be open…documents may be furnished without charge or at a reduced charge when the public governmental body determines that waiver or reduction of the fee is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to the public understanding of the operations or activities of the public governmental body and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester.”

The subject of the requested documents/records will provide significant insight into local governmental operations and activities around access to public records/documents. The requested records are not for commercial use.

If fees cannot be waived, I am willing to pay all reasonable duplication expenses incurred in the processing this Missouri Sunshine Law request. If fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of completing fulfilment of my request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Hawkins

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Memphis - Hana Haden
communications.communication:
Dear Ellen (no last name provided):
The University of Memphis is in receipt of a public records request submitted through Muckrock, a third-party proxy automated request service.
Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act (TPRA), Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503(a)(2)(A), the right of access to inspect or receive copies of public records is limited to citizens of the State of Tennessee.  Agencies may require reasonable proof of Tennessee citizenship prior to producing records.
In this instance:

  *   The request was submitted by a third-party proxy service rather than an individual requestor; and
  *   Upon information and belief, the proxy service “volunteer” has no independent knowledge of, or involvement in, the underlying request; and
  *   No information has been provided identifying a Tennessee citizen on whose behalf the request was submitted, nor has proof of Tennessee citizenship been supplied.
Because the University cannot confirm that the request is being made by a citizen of Tennessee, the request is denied pursuant to Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503(a).
Please do not continue to send automated emails to this account – this request is denied.
Thank you,
Darren Wibberding
Public Records Request Coordinator

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Audit - National Lookups
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

On March 9th, your agency issued a press release about the use of Flock Safety cameras by federal agencies. Please provide the following information relating to your Flock Safety system: 

1) Please provide any internal audits, reports, or memos written about the audit your agency conducted after finding out the about the "National Lookup" configuration issue in Flock Safety. 

2) Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3) Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4) A list of network share settings that include:
 - 4a. Flock networks shared with me

title: Metro Transit / Bi State Development Agency – Public Safety Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Metro Transit / Bi State Development Agency – Public Safety Records
communications.communication:
Attention: Custodian of Records/ Chief Legal Counsel 

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Legal Authority and Purpose:
This request is made pursuant to the Missouri Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, RSMo, which mandates public access to “records of public governmental bodies” unless specifically exempt (§ 610.011, RSMo).

The Bi State Development Agency (BSD), as operator of Metro Transit, performs core public transportation functions in Missouri using significant public funds. Accordingly, records concerning public safety, incident reports, and operational alerts on Metro Transit routes within Missouri are subject to disclosure under Chapter 610.

This request seeks to improve public transparency regarding safety on Metro Transit routes, specifically for passengers and community members using public transportation in the St. Louis region.

Requested Documents/Records:
Please provide copies of all records, electronic or paper, related to public safety, security incidents, alerts, or communications for the following specific Metro Transit bus routes in St. Louis, Missouri:
•	11 Chippewa Route
•	41 Lee Route 
•	74 Florissant Route 


Date Range: November 1, 2024 – the final date of completion for all processing of this request. 


A. Incident and Public Safety Logs
•	All incident reports, logs, or records generated by Metro Transit staff, security personnel, law enforcement, or other partners for these routes. 
•	Include events such as suspicious behavior, threats, assaults, disturbances, trespassing, medical emergencies, police responses, or other safety-related events. 

B. Rider Safety Alerts and Notifications
•	Public and internal notifications sent to riders or staff related to safety incidents on these routes. 
•	Drafts, emails, or records showing the creation and dissemination of alerts. 

C. Internal Communications About Safety and Security
•	Emails, memoranda, or reports between Metro Transit and law enforcement or other partners regarding these routes involving any previously listed events (suspicious behavior, threats, assaults, etc.). 
•	Any discussion of patterns, trends, or security planning affecting the 11 Chippewa, 41 Lee and 74 Florissant routes. 

D. Security Assessments, Audits, or Evaluations
•	Security reviews, audits, or assessments specific to the 11 Chippewa, 41 Lee and 74 Florissant routes, including risk mitigation or safety recommendations. 


Requested Format:
I am respectfully seeking all documents/records through MuckRock.com.

I am requesting that documents/records be sent:
•	Electronically, preferably as a searchable PDF file attachment or native electronic files (Excel, CSV). 
•	Records organized by route and date range, with an index identifying content. 
•	Redact only legally exempt personal information; provide the remainder of records. 

Per Section 610.024, RSMo, “If a public record contains material which is not exempt from disclosure as well as material which is exempt from disclosure, the public governmental body shall separate the exempt and nonexempt material and make the nonexempt material available for examination and copying.”

If portions of the requested documents/records are closed due to exemption, please separate, omit or redact only the closed portions and provide me with all segregable portions of the documents/records. I also request that you please provide a detailed justification for each redaction or omission, and cite the specific exemption used for each redacted or omitted portion of the original document/records.

Fee Waiver/Public Interest/Public Release:
As the records sought detail the operations and activities of an entity providing services on behalf of local government, I also request that fees be waived as this request is in the public’s interest. I am seeking the records to analyze, inform and freely release to members of the public as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com.

As stated in Section 610.022, RSMo, “public records shall be presumed to be open…documents may be furnished without charge or at a reduced charge when the public governmental body determines that waiver or reduction of the fee is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to the public understanding of the operations or activities of the public governmental body and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester.”  The subject of the requested documents/records will provide considerably increased insight into passenger and personnel safety during Metro Transit operations. The requested records are not for commercial use.

If fees cannot be waived, I am willing to pay all reasonable duplication expenses incurred in the processing this Missouri Sunshine Law request. However, as I am a member of the public and requesting documents specifically for the purpose of increasing the public’s knowledge of governmental functioning, I will administratively appeal any denial of my request for a fee waiver. If fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of completing fulfilment of my request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Hawkins
